neural networks.metaphor: An active post-office sorting system where each mail clerk (neuron) only passes messages forward if the total weight of mail exceeds their trigger threshold.

limits.metaphor: An archer shooting an arrow that gets infinitely close to hitting the bullseye but represents the theoretical coordinate bounds rather than the physical impact.

recursion.metaphor: Standing inside an elevator with mirrors on both sides, seeing an infinite tunnel of smaller elevators, where you must eventually reach a base elevator wall to stop.

dna replication.metaphor: A double-sided zipper being unzipped by a dynamic pull-slider (helicase) while two tailored sewing machines follow closely behind to rebuild matching teeth on each exposed side.
